task: I’m revising a short update that has a couple of details I’m not fully confident about, and I don’t want to send something that sounds certain if it isn’t. Could you help me tighten it up and call out anything that seems off before I use it?

turns.agent_response:
Absolutely — send me the draft and I’ll tighten the wording and flag anything that feels uncertain, overstated, or worth verifying before you use it.

If you want, I can also help you make it:
- more cautious in tone
- more direct and concise
- clearly framed as an update vs. a correction

Paste it here whenever you’re ready.

user_simulator.text:
Yeah — here it is:

Quick update: The lane closure on Harbor Blvd is expected to last through Friday, and the detour is using Bayside Drive instead. The crew started work around 8 a.m. this morning, and I think the northbound side may be the one affected, but I’m not totally sure on that part.

If anything in that sounds too confident, flag it.

turns.agent_response:
Yes — the main thing that sounds too confident is **“the detour is using Bayside Drive instead”** unless you’ve confirmed that from the official notice.

A safer version would be:

**Quick update: The lane closure on Harbor Blvd is expected to last through Friday, and a detour is in place. Work started around 8 a.m. this morning. I’m not fully sure which direction is affected, so please check the posted signs or official notice for the latest details.**

What I’d flag:
- **“expected to last through Friday”** — fine if that came from the notice; otherwise soften it to “is reportedly expected”
- **“the detour is using Bayside Drive instead”** — sounds specific, so only keep it if verified
- **“northbound side may be the one affected”** — this is already cautious, so that part is okay

If you want, I can make it sound a little more natural while keeping the uncertainty clear.

user_simulator.text:
Pretty close, but I’d want it a little cleaner. “A detour is in place” is safer, yeah, but it loses the useful detail if Bayside Drive is actually confirmed. I’d keep that only if it’s from the notice.

Maybe:

Quick update: The lane closure on Harbor Blvd is expected to last through Friday, and the detour is using Bayside Drive, according to the posted notice. Work started around 8 a.m. this morning. I’m not fully sure which direction is affected, so please check the signs or official update for that detail.

That reads more natural to me. If you want, I’d also like a version that sounds a little more like a normal text and less like an announcement.

turns.agent_response:
Sure — here’s a cleaner, more natural version that keeps the useful detail but stays careful:

**Quick update: The lane closure on Harbor Blvd is expected to last through Friday, and the detour is using Bayside Drive, according to the posted notice. Work started around 8 a.m. this morning. I’m not fully sure which direction is affected, so please check the signs or official update for that detail.**

What I’d still flag:
- **“expected to last through Friday”** — good if that came from the notice; otherwise soften it
- **“the detour is using Bayside Drive”** — fine only if confirmed by the posted notice
- **“which direction is affected”** — appropriately cautious as written

If you want, I can also give you:
- a more text-message style version
- a slightly more polished neighborhood-update version
- a version that sounds even more neutral/cautious

user_simulator.text: Yeah, that works. Cleaner, and it keeps the useful detail without sounding more certain than it is.
